summary: One of the largest consumer credit bureau breaches in history. SSNs, DOBs, and financial data exposed.
State-linked (Russia/APT29) supply chain attack affecting US government agencies and Fortune 500 companies.
Attacker gained access to Uber's internal tools, Slack, and HackerOne reports via social engineering of an employee.

attack_vector: Social engineering via LinkedIn + ransomware deployment
summary: Scattered Spider group disrupted MGM casino operations for 10+ days. Estimated $100M+ in losses.

attack_vector: Third-party HVAC vendor credentials → POS malware
summary: Attackers entered via a third-party vendor. Malware scraped credit card data from point-of-sale systems.

attack_vector: Inherited breach via Starwood acquisition (undetected since 2014)
summary: Breach traced back to 2014 Starwood acquisition. Passport numbers, payment data, and travel history exposed.

attack_vector: Brute force attack on testing environment
summary: SSNs, driver's license data, and IMEI numbers exposed. T-Mobile's fifth breach since 2018.

attack_vector: Misconfigured AWS WAF exploited by former AWS employee
summary: Insider threat combined with cloud misconfiguration. One of the largest financial sector breaches.
Largest known breach by volume. Reduced Yahoo's acquisition price by $350M during Verizon deal.
Wiped Sony's internal systems, leaked unreleased films, executive emails, and employee SSNs.
Names, addresses, phone numbers, and email addresses exposed. No financial data stolen.
Full user database exposed. eBay asked all users to reset passwords. No financial data taken.

attack_vector: Stolen vendor credentials + custom POS malware
summary: Six months of undetected POS malware. Larger card theft than Target breach.

attack_vector: Unauthenticated API endpoint exposed to internet
summary: Australia's second-largest telco. Passports, driver's licenses, and Medicare numbers exposed.

attack_vector: Social engineering of IT help desk (Scattered Spider)
summary: Paid ~$15M ransom quietly. Breach disclosed same week as MGM attack by same group.
Privately held. Shut down 5,500 miles of pipeline. US declared national emergency. Paid $4.4M ransom.
Attackers had access since 2020. Targeted journalist emails at WSJ, NY Post, and News Corp HQ.
Forced manual operations across manufacturing. Caused product shortages and $356M revenue impact in Q1 FY2024.
